texts.full_text: Lorenzo Monaco (born Piero di Giovanni; 1370 – c. 1425) was an Italian painter of the late Gothic-early Renaissance age. He was born Piero di Giovanni in Siena. Little is known about his youth, apart from the fact that he was apprenticed in Florence. He was influenced by Giotto and his followers Spinello Aretino and Agnolo Gaddi. In 1390 he joined the Camaldolese monastery of Santa Maria degli Angeli. He was thenceforth generally known as Lorenzo Monaco (English: "Lawrence the Monk"). texts.full_text: common ground for artists creating and performing pop-rock, funk, soul, and hip-hop. Yet, there in the middle, connecting them, is that small electronic band from Düsseldorf: four men who blurred the line between human capability and digital precision. Ironically, the band that would go on to inspire musicians from all genres never aspired to make names for themselves individually. Seeking only to expand the scope of musical technology and to comment on a changing world, Kraftwerk’s music grew to be so profound that they came to be known as the “Beatles of electronic music.” At the onset of accessible computer technology in the early ‘80s, Kraftwerk released Computer World. texts.preview_head: u're trying to create a text truncation effect with an ellipsis inside a box, but you're encountering issues because `overflow: hidden` typically doesn't work well with inline elements. To achieve the
texts.preview_tail: event line breaks. The `overflow: hidden;` property hides any part of the text that overflows the container's width, and `text-overflow: ellipsis;` adds an ellipsis at the end of the truncated text. I
texts.full_text:
u're trying to create a text truncation effect with an ellipsis inside a box, but you're encountering issues because `overflow: hidden` typically doesn't work well with inline elements. To achieve the desired effect, you can use a combination of CSS properties that make the element block-level or inline-block and then apply the necessary styles for text overflow. Here's how you can do it: ```css .box-text { background-color: #f0f0f0; // width: 200px; // white-space: nowrap; // overflow: hidden; // text-overflow: ellipsis; // display: inline-block; /* Makes the element block-level but allows it to sit within a line of text */ } ``` And here's how you can use this class in your HTML: ```html
This is some short text
This is some longer text that will be truncated with an ellipsis if it exceeds the width of the box
``` In this example, `.box-text` has a fixed width and uses `white-space: nowrap;` to prevent line breaks. The `overflow: hidden;` property hides any part of the text that overflows the container's width, and `text-overflow: ellipsis;` adds an ellipsis at the end of the truncated text. I
